title: Why Gendo is always wearing tinted glasses
display_title: Why Gendo is always wearing tinted glasses
wikitext:
[]: Why Gendo is always wearing tinted glasses             Quote Post by Naz » Sat Jun 30, 2007 7:40 pm I was watching the series again this last week with a friend who had never seen it, and I brought up the fact that both the fifth and first children had red eyes and were likely albino. Later on I mentioned that the embryo in Gendo's hand was indeed adam. She proceeded to ask me the following question: "Oh so is that why Gendo is always wearing sunglasses? He has red eyes too?" Haha, just a thought, but could it be that Gendo becomes albino after implanting the embryo of adam into his hand? He could have dyed his hair. The major issue here is that I believe there are several scenes in late episodes taken of Gendo's side. If you zoomed in I'm sure his eyes are not coloured red. Just kind of a cool idea, if there are no specific shots of his eyes late in the series. I'll throw in a bit more before people start flaming. Another problem with this idea is that Gendo has worn tinted glasses almost the entire series (his previous glasses had no tint). Gendo has worn gloves and glasses since the start; could it have been possible that he had been expecting to put the embryo in his hand and for his eyes to change colour? I'll buy the gloves, but the eyes? Anyway, take what you will. Let the flaming commence!

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sat Jun 30, 2007 7:49 pm I bet he wears tinted glasses because he's hardcore as fuck. </didn't read thread>

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jun 30, 2007 8:03 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: He wears tinted glasses because he's hardcore as fuck.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Jun 30, 2007 8:33 pm I remember some scenes of flashbacks with Gendo-kun in a younger day where he did not wear tinted glasses or even basic glasses at all (far-sighted maybe?).

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Jun 30, 2007 8:34 pm Possibly to prevent people from seeing his eyes and keep people from reading his expression? You can keep a straight face, but your eyes will still tell it all.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jun 30, 2007 8:49 pm 1) Someone cannot become albino, it's congenital. 2) Agree with Trigger.

[]:             Quote Post by Naz » Sat Jun 30, 2007 8:53 pm BrikHaus wrote: 1) Someone cannot become albino, it's congenital.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Jun 30, 2007 9:01 pm BrikHaus wrote: 1) Someone cannot become albino, it's congenital.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jun 30, 2007 9:15 pm BrikHaus wrote: 1) Someone cannot become albino, it's congenital.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jun 30, 2007 10:31 pm Gendou wore glasses back in the early days; so obviously needed them. And anyway, Rei and Kaworu are NOT "albino", their pale and have red eyes because of whole Lilith/Adam deal; or so I've heard. (...Rachel, of course.)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jun 30, 2007 11:02 pm There isn't anything that says they aren't albino.

[]:             Quote Post by Naz » Sat Jun 30, 2007 11:08 pm I was always under the assumption they were albino and that Rei's hair went blue because of some problem with the reiquarium/drugs in her room

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jun 30, 2007 11:08 pm BrikHaus wrote: There isn't anything that says they aren't albino.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Jun 30, 2007 11:15 pm Reichu wrote: I'd say they're about as albino as Adam and Lilith are. Sometimes red eyes and pale skin are just red eyes and pale skin.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jun 30, 2007 11:28 pm @brik: I was just gonna say; wasn't there a thread about this already and didn't it get locked? ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jun 30, 2007 11:34 pm The albinism thing is overdone. Hell, I have albinism. You wouldn't know it just looking at me, would you?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Jul 01, 2007 10:53 am maybe they're just very pale. I mean, they spend a lot of time not in the sun, but in labs and in various yellow-tinted liquids, and what not. Throughout most of their lives they live underground, in secret NERV and SEELE bases. I guess that takes away their complexion. I mean, Naoko Akagi writes to young Ritsuko that she is underground most of the time, and her skin is pretty pale. But, Yui's skin is pale as well....and the scenes featuring her physical presence are in the sun...damn don't know how to explain that. Well, I can guess that those scenes are when she is out of the building? The series doesn't say how much sun exposure a typical employee at SEELE gets...oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 01, 2007 12:46 pm I think they're "just very pale", as well (especially Kaworu, with the eggnog-like complexion), but not due to any lack of sun. Rather, IMO, it's intended to link them to the Seeds whose souls they possess. We know Lilith is ghostly white. We can't be sure about Adam, but the suggestion is there that she is similarly wan. In terms of paleness, there isn't much of a contest between Rei and Yui. Here's a group pic I colored, taking great pains to get the different characters' complexions as correct as I could: http://www.evacommentary.org/images_fanart/meguro-fukuzo_group-color_reichu.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Kaminoyouni » Sun Jul 01, 2007 5:20 pm i'm just waiting for someone to put Rei and Kaworu to be put in a 'Pale Force' joke

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Jul 01, 2007 7:37 pm BobBQ wrote: The albinism thing is overdone. Hell, I have albinism. You wouldn't know it just looking at me, would you?
